title: List Active Users
prompt:
The users table tracks signups.

Return id and name of every active user, ordered by id.
schema:
CREATE TABLE users(id INT, name TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO users VALUES(1,'Alice',1),(2,'Bob',0),(3,'Carol',1),(4,'Dan',1);
solution: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE is_active = 1 ORDER BY id;
hints: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE is_active = 1 ORDER BY id;

title: Products In Price Range
prompt: Return name of products priced between 10 and 50 inclusive, ordered alphabetically.
schema:
CREATE TABLE products(id INT, name TEXT, price REAL);
INSERT INTO products VALUES(1,'Pen',5),(2,'Book',12),(3,'Lamp',45),(4,'Desk',120),(5,'Mug',10);
solution: SELECT name FROM products WHERE price BETWEEN 10 AND 50 ORDER BY name;

title: Names Starting With A
prompt: Return name of employees whose name starts with A, sorted A-Z.
schema:
CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT);
INSERT INTO employees VALUES(1,'Alice'),(2,'Adam'),(3,'Bob'),(4,'Anna'),(5,'Carl');
solution: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE name LIKE 'A%' ORDER BY name;
hints: A% matches anything starting with A.

title: Users Without Email
prompt: Return id and name of users with a NULL email.
schema:
CREATE TABLE users(id INT, name TEXT, email TEXT);
INSERT INTO users VALUES(1,'A','a@x.com'),(2,'B',NULL),(3,'C',NULL),(4,'D','d@x.com');
starter: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE ___;
solution: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE email IS NULL ORDER BY id;

title: Users In Selected Cities
prompt: Return id, name of users in Paris or Lyon, ordered by id.
schema:
CREATE TABLE users(id INT, name TEXT, city TEXT);
INSERT INTO users VALUES(1,'A','Paris'),(2,'B','Berlin'),(3,'C','Lyon'),(4,'D','Paris');
starter: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE ___;
solution: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE city IN ('Paris','Lyon') ORDER BY id;

title: Non-Cancelled Orders
prompt: Return order_id of orders whose status is anything except cancelled, ordered by id.
schema:
CREATE TABLE orders(order_id INT, status TEXT);
INSERT INTO orders VALUES(1,'paid'),(2,'cancelled'),(3,'pending'),(4,'paid');
solution: SELECT order_id FROM orders WHERE status <> 'cancelled' ORDER BY order_id;
